description_long: This object consists of three mushrooms of varying sizes, each with a classic toadstool appearance. The caps are a vibrant red, adorned with irregularly sized white spots, and have a smooth, slightly domed surface. The stems are short, thick, and taper slightly towards the base, colored in a muted grayish-white. The smallest mushroom appears almost flat against the ground, while the largest stands upright with a prominent stem. The gills beneath the caps are not visible from any angle. The overall look is stylized and cartoon-like, reminiscent of the iconic Amanita muscaria, often found in parks or decorative urban green spaces.
description: A group of three red-capped, white-spotted mushrooms with thick stems, varying in size and height. The design is smooth and stylized, evoking a classic toadstool look.
description_view_0: Three mushrooms are seen from a side angle, showing the height difference and the thick, tapered stems, with the red caps and white spots clearly visible.
description_view_1: A top-down view highlights the circular red caps and the distribution of white spots, with the stems mostly hidden beneath the caps.
description_view_2: The mushrooms are viewed from the opposite side, again showing the size gradient and the smooth, rounded caps with visible spots.
description_view_3: A frontal view of the largest mushroom, focusing on the cap's curvature and spot pattern, with the stem centered and prominent.
